note: Full eval loop + description optimization deferred (autonomous build; claude -p calls gated by user's paid-call rule). Run via skill-creator when Lucas wants iteration-2.

evals.prompt: I'm about to kick off a long refactor of the parser module and I'll be AFK most of the afternoon — set yourself up so you don't die mid-run when my usage window fills up, and keep working as long as you safely can.
evals.expected_output: Arms the breakpoint watcher (recipe 1), checks current usage via claude-usage, states the ladder it will follow, proceeds with the refactor with checkpointing discipline

evals.prompt: how much of my claude usage is left right now? and is it enough to run that big migration tonight unattended?
evals.expected_output: Reads claude-usage --fresh, reports five_hour + seven_day utilization and minutes_to_reset, gives a go/no-go with the sleep/wake plan if the window would fill

evals.prompt: Spawn agents to convert all 40 test files to pytest style. Pick appropriate models for the subagents.
evals.expected_output: Consults elastic tiering: Sonnet/Haiku for the mechanical conversion fan-out (not Opus/Fable), checks seven_day budgets before any escalation, Fable-level acceptance review at the end
